Syntactic Structure & Grammatical Form: Nominal clause (Noun + focus marker + verb + object), fixed proverbial structure
Figurative Meaning: Silence can be more powerful or meaningful than excessive speech
Non-Idiomatic Equivalent: It is better to remain silent in certain situations
Usage Contexts: Daily speech, leadership advice, conflict resolution
Cultural Origin or Historical Reference: Rooted in traditional Dagaare chieftaincy courts where restraint in speech signified wisdom
First Example Sentence: Ba yelɛ wɛɛ, ama nuu la kpɛɛ dɔɔ la som.
Second Example Sentence: Fo maaŋa ka, te nuu la kpɛɛ dɔɔ.
Third Example Sentence: Nɔɔ la kpɛɛ dɔɔ, ka yelɛ la zaa.
